description: This figure presents a density-composition plot showing the density reduction of Fe-Al and Fe-Mn-Al-C alloy systems as a function of aluminum content. The x-axis represents Al content in wt.% ranging from 0 to 12, while the y-axis shows density in g·cm⁻³ from 6.5 to 8.0. Two datasets are plotted: blue circles connected by a line represent the binary Fe-Al system, starting from approximately 7.8 g·cm⁻³ at 0% Al and decreasing nearly linearly to about 6.85 g·cm⁻³ at 10.5% Al; red triangles connected by a line represent the Fe-Mn-Al-C system containing 14 wt.% Mn, starting from approximately 7.1 g·cm⁻³ at 7% Al and decreasing to about 6.7 g·cm⁻³ at 11% Al. Both curves demonstrate that increasing Al content progressively lowers the alloy density. At comparable Al contents, the Fe-Mn-Al-C system exhibits lower density than the Fe-Al system, indicating that Mn provides an additional slight density reduction effect as noted in the caption. The figure supports the core conclusion that Al is highly effective in reducing steel density—approximately 8 wt.% Al causing roughly 10% density reduction—and that Fe-Mn-Al-C multicomponent systems offer greater lightweighting potential than binary Fe-Al alloys for automotive applications.
